tool.name: sourmash sketch dna
tool.description: compute a MinHash DNA signature for an assembly
tool.command:
#set $in_ext = 'fasta.gz' if str($input.ext) == 'fasta.gz' else 'fasta' ln -sf '$input' 'input.${in_ext}' &&
sourmash sketch dna -p k=31,scaled=1000 --name '$input.element_identifier' -o '$output' 'input.${in_ext}'

param.name: input
param.label: Input assembly (FASTA)
param.help: Nucleotide FASTA for one assembly, plain or gzipped. One DNA signature is produced.

tool.help:
**sourmash sketch dna** — compute a scaled MinHash signature for an assembly.
This tool runs `sourmash sketch dna` to build a compact [scaled MinHash](https://sourmash.readthedocs.io) signature from a nucleotide FASTA assembly. The signature is a small JSON document that fingerprints the k-mer content of the genome and can be compared cheaply against other signatures (`sourmash compare`) to estimate pairwise genome similarity — the first step in assembling a non-redundant pangenome panel.
The sketch parameters are fixed at **k=31, scaled=1000**, the standard settings for genome-scale comparison; nothing is exposed.
-----
**Input**
- `Input assembly (FASTA)` — nucleotide FASTA (plain or gzipped) for a single assembly. At the workflow level this tool maps over a collection of assemblies, producing one signature per assembly.
**Output**
- `signature` — a sourmash signature (`.sig`), emitted here as `json` (a sourmash signature is JSON). Consumable directly by `sourmash compare`.
-----
sourmash computes MinHash sketches of DNA k-mers for fast similarity and containment estimation between sequencing datasets and genomes.
